parts.answer: • equation Examples of equation (Ba(NO ) ∙2H O → ) BaO + 2NO + ½O + 2H O 2(Ba(NO ) ∙2H O → ) 2BaO + 4NO + O + 4H O Ignore state symbols even if incorrect Allow multiples if the candidate has put a number in front of the Ba(NO ) ∙2H O (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark (formula) • SO 2− (1) (positive test result) • white precipitate / white solid (1) Mark independently Ignore name Allow ionic equations that include SO 2− Do not award other ionic equations Allow white ppt(e) Do not award if other chemical tests are mentioned If the precipitate is given as AgCl do not award this mark (2) (Total for Question 1 = 11 marks) Number Additional Guidance Mark

parts.answer: • axes correct way round and linear scales to cover more than half the graph paper • axes labelled with units (allow even if axes are the wrong way round) • all points plotted correctly (allow ± small square) (1) (1) (1) Example of graph: Ignore any hand-drawn lines between the points Do not award ‘T’ for ‘time’ for the label on the time axis. (3) Number Additional Guidance Mark • extrapolated line drawn • correct value for ∆T (1) (1) Diagonal line extrapolated back to at least 2.5 minutes ∆T = (29.3 – 21.0 =) 8.3 ( o C) TE on their line M2 dependent on the temperature difference being measured at 2.5 minutes Ignore other lines drawn. (2) Number Additional Guidance Mark

An answer that makes reference to the following point: • suitable reason for lower exothermic value Heat loss (to the surroundings / from the apparatus) Mass of solution is more than 100 g Density is more than 1 g cm Specific heat capacity of not 4.18 J g −1 o C −1 Do not award if a correct answer is given together with an answer with incorrect chemistry. Do not award reference to non-standard conditions Do not award transfer errors/incomplete reaction (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark • calculation of enthalpy change Example of calculation ∆H = (−69.4 − +15.7 =) −85.1 (kJ mol −1 ) Allow value shown on the cycle. Ignore units even if incorrect. Ignore working even if incorrect. (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following point: • suitable reason why value not found by experiment Examples of possible reasons Hard to add the correct amount of water Some crystals would be dissolved whilst other not fully hydrated Hard to measure the temperature (change) of a solid Ignore hard to measure the temperature (change) of magnesium sulfate unqualified (1) (Total for Question 2 = 8 marks) Number Additional Guidance Mark

parts.answer: An answer that makes reference to the following point: • concentration would be less and because the mass (of sodium hydroxide) would include the moisture Allow water for moisture Do not award refence to substances other than sodium hydroxide Do not award reference to the volume increases (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark • 9.51 – 9.55 (g) Allow 9.51 ~ 9.55 (g) Allow 9.53 ±0.02 (g) (1) Number . Additional Guidance Mark • calculation of molar mass of borax • calculation of moles of borax weighed out • calculation of moles of borax in pipetted 25.0 cm (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation Molar mass = 381.2 (g mol −1 ) n = (9.53 ÷ 381.2 =) 0.025 / 2.5 x 10 −2 (mol) n = (0.025 x (25 ÷ 500 =) 0.00125 / 1.25 x 10 −3 (mol) Ignore SF except 1 SF Correct answer with no working scores (3) TE throughout (3) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following point: • (from) yellow) (to) orange Do not award any other colours (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark • calculation of moles of HCl • calculation of concentration of HCl in mol dm −3 • calculation of concentration of HCl in g dm −3 to 2/3SF (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation n = (0.00125 x 2 =) 0.0025 / 2.50 x 10 −3 (mol) c = (0.0025 ÷ (16.30 ÷ 1000 =) 0.15337 / 1.5337 x 10 −1 c = (0.15337 x 36.5 = 5.5982 =) 5.60/5.6 Correct answer with no working scores (3) Allow calculations done in any order, including in one step. TE throughout and from 3(b)(i) Common wrong answers: Using 0.00125 as the moles of HCl gives 2.80/2.8 (scores 2) Dividing 0.00125 by 2 gives 1.40 /1.4 (scores 2) Using the mean of 3 titres (16.53) gives 5.52/5.5 (scores 2) (3) (Total for Question 3 = 9 marks) Number Additional Guidance Mark • HCl Ignore state symbols Ignore name Do not award if more than one answer given. (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark • carbon dioxide / CO If name and formula given then both must be correct Ignore state symbols Do not award if given with other substances (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark •

An answer that makes reference to the following points: (bromine water) • from brown/ orange / yellow • to colourless (Benedict’s / Fehling’s reagent) • from blue (solution) • to (brick) red precipitate (1) (1) (1) (1) Standalone marks Ignore layers throughout Do not award red Allow decolourises Ignore clear Do not award reference to effervescence Allow red-brown for red Allow solid / ppt(e) for precipitate Do not award brown / orange / pink for red Ignore reference to the solution turning colourless before the precipitate is formed Do not award reference to the solution turning white and the precipitate is formed Do not award silver mirror. (4) (Total for Question 4 = 11 marks) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following points: • use of gloves • use a fume cupboard / hood (1) (1) Allow ensure laboratory is well-ventilated Ignore masks, safety specs, goggles, lab coat Ignore flammability Do not award using a lower concentration or less HCl (2) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following point: • pressure / gas / CO must be released Allow to prevent the build up of pressure Ignore reference to explosions Ignore just a gas is produced Do not award reference to release air / water vapour / other gases Do not award a reference to removal of lower / aqueous layer (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following point: • cloudy to clear Allow milky for cloudy Ignore just ‘goes clear’ Ignore reference to colourless (1) Number Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following points: • (bp1) round-bottomed/pear-shaped flask • (bp2) heat • (bp3) still head with no gaps to outside and no blockages • (bp4) still head with entry to condenser • (bp5) thermometer with any part of bulb or bottom of thermometer opposite entry to condenser • (bp6) labelling (of condenser) with water in and water out • (bp7) condenser with exit for distillate or vent for

An answer that makes reference to three of the following points: • rate is inversely proportional to time • 2-chloro-2-methylpropane is tertiary and rate (of hydrolysis) is fastest/faster • 1-chloro-2-methylpropane and 1-bromo-2-methylpropane are primary (halogenoalkanes) and C-Br bonds are hydrolysed / broken faster / faster rate than C-Cl bonds (1) (1) (1) Allow any indication that a shorter time means a faster rate Ignore just restatement of times from the table without comment Allow greatest / higher for fastest Allow reference to shorter time Allow reference to shorter time Ignore reference to stability of carbocations Ignore references to bond strength of C-Br bonds and C-Cl bonds Allow reference to shorter time to hydrolyse C-Br bonds Accept reverse arguments throughout (3) (Total for Question 5 = 11 marks) TOTAL FOR PAPER = 50 MARKS
